question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition during startup by writing files into the java.io.tmpdir path (/tmp/web3) between the FileSystemUtils.deleteRecursively() and Files.createDirectories() calls, causing the node to fail initialization and crash, effectively taking it offline without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user pre-populate the /tmp/web3 directory with a deeply nested directory structure containing millions of files before node startup, causing FileSystemUtils.deleteRecursively() to consume excessive CPU and memory during cleanup(), delaying or crashing the node startup process and taking it offline?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cleanup() silently swallows IOException via System.err.printf() without halting startup, causing the node to start with a corrupted or missing tmpDir, leading to downstream failures in EVM execution that crash the node mid-operation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableAsync annotation's default thread pool by sending a flood of async-dispatched contract call requests that exhaust the shared executor thread pool, causing the node to become unresponsive and effectively shut down processing without triggering rate-limit brute force detection?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests that exploit the SpringApplication.run() auto-configuration bean initialization order, causing a bean dependency cycle or initialization failure that crashes the application context and takes the node offline?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user create a symlink at /tmp/web3 pointing to a critical system directory before node startup, causing FileSystemUtils.deleteRecursively() to follow the symlink and delete critical system files, crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the java.io.tmpdir system property resolution in cleanup() by manipulating environment variables or JVM arguments passed at startup to redirect tmpDir to a read-only filesystem, causing Files.createDirectories() to throw an IOException that is silently ignored, leaving the node in a broken state?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Import(CommonConfiguration.class) bean loading to trigger a classpath scanning vulnerability that causes the Spring context to load malicious beans from a shared classpath location, crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user fill the /tmp filesystem to 100% capacity before node startup, causing Files.createDirectories() to fail with an IOException that is silently swallowed, leaving the node unable to write temporary EVM execution files and crashing during contract processing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user send crafted HTTP requests to the /api/v1/contracts/call endpoint with maximum gas values just below the throttle threshold, consuming the gasLimitBucket tokens across multiple nodes simultaneously, causing all nodes to reject legitimate transactions and effectively shutting down >= 30% of processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-atomic nature of the deleteRecursively() followed by createDirectories() sequence in cleanup() to inject a malicious file into tmpDir between the two operations, causing the EVM to load and execute attacker-controlled bytecode from the tmp directory?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication component scan to trigger auto-configuration of a vulnerable third-party library included in the classpath, causing a remote code execution or crash during node startup?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cleanup() uses System.getProperty('java.io.tmpdir', '/tmp/web3') — where the default fallback is /tmp/web3 — to predict the exact tmp path and pre-stage a denial-of-service payload that causes node startup to hang indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user send a single crafted request with gas set to exactly Long.MAX_VALUE to overflow the scaleGas() calculation in ThrottleProperties, bypassing the gasLimitBucket throttle and consuming unbounded EVM resources that crash the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableAsync thread pool by sending requests that trigger long-running async tasks (e.g., complex EVM contract execution), exhausting the async executor queue and causing the node's Spring context to become unresponsive, effectively shutting it down?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the tmpDir path (/tmp/web3) being world-writable in a shared container environment to write a large number of files into it during node operation, causing subsequent node restarts to hang during FileSystemUtils.deleteRecursively() and preventing the node from coming back online?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a crafted Content-Encoding: gzip header containing a zip bomb payload to the /api/v1/contracts/call endpoint, causing the ContentCachingRequestWrapper in LoggingFilter to decompress the payload into memory and trigger an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the requestTimeout of 10 seconds (Web3Properties.requestTimeout) by sending requests that intentionally consume exactly 9.9 seconds of EVM execution time, keeping all worker threads occupied and starving the node of capacity to process legitimate requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SpringApplication.run() Spring Boot auto-configuration to trigger loading of a DataSource bean that attempts to connect to a database, and by making the database unavailable, cause the node to fail startup and remain offline?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the IOException catch block in cleanup() that only prints to System.err without throwing, allowing the node to start in a degraded state where EVM execution fails for all requests due to missing tmpDir, effectively shutting down all contract processing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user send a large number of concurrent requests each with gas=10001 (just above the GAS_SCALE_FACTOR of 10000), causing each request to consume exactly 1 token from the gasLimitBucket, draining it faster than it refills and starving all other nodes sharing the same throttle configuration?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication auto-scan of the org.hiero.mirror.web3 package to trigger initialization of a bean that reads from an external configuration source, and by poisoning that source, cause the node to crash during startup?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cleanup() is called before SpringApplication.run() to create a timing window where the node is partially initialized, and by sending requests during this window, cause the node to crash due to uninitialized beans?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LoggingFilter's GZIPOutputStream compression of large payloads by sending requests with payloads that are pathologically incompressible, causing the compression step to consume excessive CPU and memory, degrading node performance to the point of effective shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ContentCachingRequestWrapper buffer size (maxPayloadLogSize * 10 = 3000 bytes by default) by sending requests with exactly 3000 bytes of body, causing the wrapper to buffer the entire payload in heap memory for every concurrent request, leading to OOM when many requests arrive simultaneously?
